name: Snuggtaskeberg
description: Trevligt litet boulderområde som ligger i Törnskogen i Sollentuna kommun. Området är uppdelat på en handfull väggar och i dagsläget är närmare 80 problem gjorda. Samtliga problem i Snuggtaskeberg toppas ut. En hel del high-balls finns så det rekommenderas att ta med kompetent spottare om man vill försöka på samtliga problem.

sections.title: Allmänt
sections.body: Trevligt litet boulderområde som ligger i Törnskogen i Sollentuna kommun. Området är uppdelat på en handfull väggar och i dagsläget är närmare 80 problem gjorda. Samtliga problem i Snuggtaskeberg toppas ut. En hel del high-balls finns så det rekommenderas att ta med kompetent spottare om man vill försöka på samtliga problem.

sections.title: Historik
sections.body: Snuggtaskebergs klätterhistoria är liksom många andra mindre områden bortom de stora klätterstråken höljt i dunkel. Någon form av aktivitet har bedrivits, det vet man, men inga dokument har återfunnits där problem beskrivits. Under 2003-2004 återupptäcktes området av en ny generation bouldrare som borstade fram vägg efter vägg och numera är det mesta självklara gjort, det som återstår är de hårdaste linjerna som i vissa fall är smått utopiska.

sections.title: Bil
sections.body: Från Danderydsvägen svänger man in på Frestavägen. Efter ca 800 meter tar man vänster in på Båtmansvägen och sedan höger på Gammelvägen. Följ Gammelvägen tills dess slut. Där finns parkering invid Turebergs klubbstuga.

sections.title: Kommunal transport
sections.body: Tag buss 527 från Sollentuna station mot Vaxmora. Kliv av vid Båtmansvägen. Gå ca 700 meter tills du kommer fram till parkeringsplatsen vid klubbstugan.

sections.title: Hitta från parkeringen
sections.body: Följ stigen från parkeringen tills huvudväggen skymtar på vänster sida. Orientera er sedan med hjälp av översiktskartan för att nå de andra väggarna.

sections.title: Huvudväggen
sections.body: Den största väggen i området och den vägg som har flest problem. Väggen är uppdelad i flera nivåer. Väggen har varierande kvalitet. Vissa delar är lite spröd, andra mycket fastare. Gröna Lund är ett problem som man inte bör missa på denna vägg.

sections.title: DDT-väggen
sections.body: Mycket fin vägg med lite svårare problem. Här återfinns godbitar som Paranoid mongoloid, Megaplume och Marmaris.

sections.title: Svaväggen
sections.body: Vägg strax bortom DDT-väggen. Har både vertikala problem och sva-problem.

sections.title: Solväggen
sections.body: Trevlig vägg som ligger uppe på platån ovanför Huvudväggen och DDT-väggen. Två fina problem gör klippan väl värd ett besök.

sections.title: Höga väggarna
sections.body: Samlingsnamn på de klippor som ligger längts bort av de vi hittat. En riktigt hög highball finns här, men också många lägre problem.

sections.title: Revolverväggen
sections.body: Liten fin vägg som ligger bortanför Höga Väggarna. Fem problem är gjorda och några till går nog att få ut av denna granitvägg.

sections.title: Koreaväggen
sections.body: Längre bort mot stora vägen finns ytterligare en liten vägg med par problem gjorda. Senaste tillskottet, ej helt rensad ännu.

Hh i undercling, vh på crimp, gå mot toppen, mantla.

routes.name: Ny
routes.description: Hög.

routes.name: Sista dagen på jorden
routes.description: Insteg någon meter till vänster om problem nr7 ut på väggen till H följ diagonala sprickan åt V.och till topps. Rammla inte ner. Ledgraderad.

images.caption: Magnus Lennartsson på Slicka uppåt - Sparka neråt (5+), Snuggtaskeberg 2006. Foto: Mattis Wide
Petter Norberg på Kim Il Sung (5+), Snuggtaskeberg 2007. Foto: Mattis Wide

description.text: A pleasant little bouldering area in Törnskogen, Sollentuna municipality. The area is spread across a handful of walls, with nearly 80 problems climbed to date. All problems at Snuggtaskeberg top out. There are quite a few highballs, so bringing a competent spotter is recommended if you want to try every problem.

body.text: A pleasant little bouldering area in Törnskogen, Sollentuna municipality. The area is spread across a handful of walls, with nearly 80 problems established to date. All problems at Snuggtaskeberg top out. There are quite a few highballs, so bringing a competent spotter is recommended if you want to attempt every problem.

Like many smaller areas away from the main climbing centres, Snuggtasberg's climbing history is shrouded in mystery. Some activity is known to have taken place, but no documents describing any problems have been found. In 2003–2004, the area was rediscovered by a new generation of boulderers, who brushed out wall after wall. Most of the obvious lines have now been climbed; what remains are the hardest lines, some of which are almost impossibly difficult.

From Danderydsvägen, turn onto Frestavägen. After about 800 metres, turn left onto Båtmansvägen, then right onto Gammelvägen. Follow Gammelvägen to the end. Parking is available beside Tureberg's clubhouse.
Take bus 527 from Sollentuna station towards Vaxmora. Get off at Båtmansvägen. Walk for about 700 metres until you reach the car park by the clubhouse.
Follow the path from the car park until the Main Wall comes into view on the left. Then use the overview map to find the other walls.

The largest wall in the area and the one with the most problems. The wall is split across several levels and varies in quality. Some sections are slightly friable, while others are much more solid. Gröna Lund is a problem not to be missed on this wall.
A very fine wall with slightly harder problems. Classics here include Paranoid mongoloid, Megaplume and Marmaris.
A wall just beyond DDT Wall, with both vertical and slab problems.
A pleasant wall on the plateau above Main Wall and DDT Wall. Two fine problems make the crag well worth a visit.
The collective name for the furthest crags we have found. There is one very high highball here, as well as many lower problems.
A small, fine wall beyond High Walls. Five problems have been established, and this granite wall probably has a few more to offer.
Further towards the main road is another small wall with a couple of established problems. This is the latest addition and has not yet been fully cleaned.

description.text: Take the edge, climb up and mantle.
Small holds, then on to the lip.
Easy arete with a slightly delicate finish.
Mantle onto the starting ledge, reach a hold with your left hand, then climb to the lip and mantle again.
Easy double mantle to the right of Dubbelmantlingen.
Sit start. Technical moves and jugs. Highball.
Sit start, possibly using a layback. Head towards the lip.
The crag's first ascent. Thin holds lead to the top.
The obvious line on the red section.
A pleasant warm-up mantle on good holds.
Requires quite a long reach. A committing finish where many a good climber has chosen to retreat.
Delicate footwork, then mantle. Excellent!
Highball. Fun start, good holds and a crimp at the top. Delicate.
The crack.
Start with your right hand on the crystal edge, then continue up on reasonable holds.
Start in the diagonal crack, climb to the lip and mantle.
Easy problem up to the tree.
Left-hand sidepull, right-hand crimp, then up to the top.
Sit start, reach for the lip and mantle.
Start on slopers on the lip and finish with a delicate mantle.
The crack to the right of Lättare än svår.
Start on a crimp edge, climb up on crimps and mantle.
Mantle problem.
The crack. Some mental resolve may be needed at the finish.
Start on a sidepull, climb up right to the ledge and continue to the top.
Harder variation of Vila sig i form. Start with your right hand on a crimp and your left on a sidepull/undercling. Lock off, go for the ledge, then continue to the top.
Mantle problem.
The large crack, thoroughly cleaned, fortunately, as it is harder than it first appears.
Right of the large crack, thin in the middle and scary at the finish.
Technical problem that eases towards the top.
Right of Stel som ett lik. Pleasant start.
Right hand on the sidepull, right foot on a good foothold. Take the Gaston with your left hand, move up, reach and mantle.
Left hand on a crimp, right hand in the crack. Step up, take the thin hold, reach for the top and mantle. Hard!
Start on crimps, throw for the lip and mantle out.
Start on thin flakes and catch the top. Considerably easier if you are over 185 cm tall – and considerably less fun.
The large crack.
A contrived variation of Uppochner. Reach the top without using the wall left of the crack.
Thin at the start; reach for a good edge, then easier climbing.
Sit start with a hand jam and your feet in the "cave". Follow the offwidth crack/chimney all the way up. Top out straight up.
Jump start to good holds, then straight up.
Left hand on the crystal sidepull, right hand on the sloper. Lock off and reach a small hold with your right hand. Continue to the ledge and mantle.
Follow the edge diagonally upwards, with your feet on the wall.
The slab.
No-hands variation.
Sit start, then straight up.
Right hand in the undercling, left hand on a crimp. Head for the top and mantle.
Start on small edges and follow the sloping edge diagonally up left.
Start as for Mattis - den svenske gambianen, but climb straight up after the first move.
Start via the ramp, then climb up.
Climb the thin wall. The ledge on The moisty slab is, of course, out. A very delicate section!
Start using the ledge, then climb straight up the slab just right of the crack.
Right foot on a small crystal, then step up towards the top.
Climb without using your hands.
Go up without using your hands.
Straight up.
Straight up.
On the corner.
Sit start on the corner.
Variant of 3b.
The face left of the crack.
Climb onto the slab.
Climb up to slopers and mantel out. The shelf system is off limits.
Hug the block and mantel.
Sit start, then climb the edge.
Climb the crack.
Through the roof.
Climb diagonally through the roof.
High.
Start a metre or so left of problem no. 7, head right onto the face, then follow the diagonal crack left to the top. Don't fall off. Given a route grade.
Put on big shoes. Don’t forget the picnic basket.
Only the arete for hands.
Start with a right hand on the arete and a left hand in the undercling.
Avoid touching the tree with your backside.
Start in the ledge and go up using a good hold with the right hand.
Start in the ledge and then go up to small crimps.
Left of the arete.
The arete right of Revolverkäke.
Start in the crimps and then go up.
Straight up the face, finishing on the slab.
Along the slabby arete.
